question: Does companion expert bundling reduce per-finding coordination overhead vs solo dispatch?

design.method: Compare rows-per-lesson and lessons-per-session for solo (1 MERGED lane) vs bundle (2+ MERGED lanes) sessions. Lesson detection: L-NNN in Notes field. Outlier filter: n_merged > 20.
confounds: lesson detection via notes only (partial coverage)
solo sessions may be simpler tasks

prediction_vs_actual.actual_overhead_direction: INVERTED: bundles have 2x MORE overhead per lesson
prediction_vs_actual.prediction_status: WRONG on efficiency direction, WRONG on magnitude

interpretation.primary: Bundling does NOT reduce coordination overhead per lesson — it increases it 2x. But it dramatically increases absolute lesson output per session (10x). The tradeoff is: more lanes per lesson, but far more lessons per session.
interpretation.mechanism: Bundle sessions tackle broader/harder problems requiring multiple expert perspectives. Each sub-problem gets a lane. More lanes = more overhead per lesson, but the session produces more lessons in total.
interpretation.verdict: F-EXP2 hypothesis FALSIFIED: bundling does not reduce per-finding coordination overhead. But bundles dominate solos on net session output (Cohen d=1.15). Bundling is the right default mode for throughput, not for efficiency.
interpretation.implication: Dispatch should target multi-domain bundle sessions for high-throughput periods. Solo dispatch preserves efficiency when only 1 lane is needed. The 'companion bundling reduces overhead' frame was wrong — bundling IS overhead, but overhead that pays off.

frontier_advance: PARTIAL — F-EXP2 hypothesis falsified (overhead NOT reduced), but bundling net-positive confirmed. Next: test whether specific personality pairings (idea-investigator + skeptic) yield different results than arbitrary bundling.
